title: viburnum subcordatum
intro: Viburnum subcordatum, also known as the Oregon crabapple, is a deciduous shrub native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America, characterized by its fragrant white flowers and red berries. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Viburnum subcordatum include reducing fever, soothing digestive issues, and providing relief from pain and inflammation. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be made by steeping the leaves, flowers, or roots in hot water or a solvent.
